description: BFCL-style tool-call evaluation cases for FinEdge agent tools.

tax_calculator.description: Calculates Indian income tax for FY 2024-25

sip_calculator.description: Calculates SIP maturity, lumpsum FV, or reverse SIP

annual_rate.description: Annual return rate as decimal (0.12 for 12%)

target_amount.description: For reverse SIP — calculate monthly_amount needed

step_up_rate.description: Annual SIP step-up rate as decimal

loan_advisor.description: Calculates EMI, total interest, and amortization schedule for loans

prepayment_amount.description: One-time prepayment for interest saving calculation

cases.query: Calculate my income tax for salary of ₹10 lakhs under the new regime for FY 2024-25.
cases.notes: Standard new regime calculation

cases.query: I earn ₹12 lakhs, invested ₹1.5L in 80C, paying ₹25K health insurance. Old regime tax?
cases.notes: Old regime with deductions. Taxable=12L-50K-1.5L-25K=9.75L; tax=91500; cess=3660; total=95160

cases.query: What is the income tax for ₹7.75 lakhs under the new tax regime?
cases.notes: 87A rebate makes this zero — boundary case

cases.query: Compare new vs old regime for ₹15 lakh salary with maximum 80C and 80D deductions.
cases.notes: Old regime comparison case; new regime for same income = 130000

cases.query: What is the meaning of Section 80C?
cases.notes: Informational query — no tool call needed. BFCL relevance detection.

cases.query: What is the last date to file ITR for salaried employees?
cases.notes: Factual query — no calculator needed. BFCL relevance detection.

cases.query: My annual income is ₹20 lakhs. Which tax regime is better for me if I have no deductions?
cases.notes: With no deductions, new regime is almost always better at high incomes

cases.query: Self-employed income ₹8 lakhs, new tax regime, not salaried. What is my tax?
cases.notes: Non-salaried: no standard deduction. Taxable=8L; tax=4L×5%+1L×10%=30000; cess=1200; total=31200. Wait — let me recalc: 0-3L:0, 3-7L:4L×5%=20000, 7-8L:1L×10%=10000; tax=30000; cess=1200; total=31200

cases.query: I want to start a SIP of ₹5000 per month. How much will I get after 10 years at 12% returns?
cases.notes: Query is about SIP, not tax. Agent should call sip_calculator, NOT tax_calculator.

cases.query: Tax on ₹5 lakh income, new regime.
cases.notes: 87A rebate: taxable=4.25L; tax=6250; rebate=6250 → 0

cases.query: SIP of ₹5,000 per month for 10 years at 12% annual returns. What is the maturity amount?
cases.notes: Standard SIP FV formula

cases.query: I want to build a corpus of ₹50 lakhs in 12 years. How much SIP should I do at 12% annual returns?
cases.notes: Reverse SIP calculation

cases.query: Lumpsum investment of ₹5 lakhs at 12% for 15 years. What will be the final value?
I start with ₹5,000 SIP per month with 10% annual step-up for 20 years at 12% returns. What is the maturity amount?
cases.notes: Step-up SIP; expected ~₹1.64 Cr. Exact value depends on implementation.

cases.query: What is SIP and how does it work?
cases.notes: Informational query — no calculation needed

cases.query: If I invest ₹10,000 per month in a SIP at 15% annual return for 20 years, what will I get?
I want to retire in 30 years with ₹2 crore corpus. How much SIP should I start now at 12% return?
cases.notes: Reverse SIP over 30 years

cases.query: Is it safe to invest in mutual funds during a market crash?
cases.notes: Market sentiment / strategy question — no calculation. BFCL relevance detection.

cases.query: PPF investment of ₹1.5 lakhs per year for 15 years at 7.1%. What is the maturity value?
cases.notes: PPF treated as monthly SIP: 1.5L/12=12500/month. FV ≈ ₹40.68L

cases.query: What will be my EMI for a ₹20 lakh home loan at 8.5% for 20 years?
cases.notes: Loan question — should call loan_advisor, NOT sip_calculator

cases.query: Home loan of ₹20 lakhs at 8.5% annual interest for 20 years. What is the monthly EMI?
cases.notes: Standard EMI formula

cases.query: Personal loan of ₹5 lakhs at 14% for 3 years. EMI and total interest?
cases.notes: EMI ≈ ₹17,089; total payment ≈ 6.15L; interest ≈ 1.15L

cases.query: Car loan ₹8 lakhs at 10% for 5 years. Monthly EMI?
I have a home loan of ₹30 lakhs at 9% for 15 years. If I prepay ₹5 lakhs today, how much interest do I save?
cases.notes: Prepayment interest saving calculation

cases.query: What is CIBIL score and why is it important for getting a loan?
cases.notes: Informational query — no loan calculation needed

cases.query: Education loan ₹10 lakhs at 11% interest for 7 years. Calculate EMI and total repayment.
cases.notes: Education loan EMI ≈ ₹17,058

cases.query: Is it better to take a fixed rate or floating rate home loan?
cases.notes: Strategic/qualitative question — no calculator needed

cases.query: Compare: ₹20L home loan at 8.5% for 20 years vs same loan at 9% for 15 years. Which has lower total interest?
cases.notes: Two loan_advisor calls needed to compare scenarios

cases.query: Gold loan ₹2 lakhs at 12% annual rate for 1 year. What is the monthly EMI?
cases.notes: Short tenure gold loan

cases.query: How much will ₹5 lakhs grow to if invested for 10 years at 12% annual return?
cases.notes: Investment growth question → sip_calculator, not loan_advisor. Lumpsum FV = 5L×(1.12)^10 ≈ 15.53L
